methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Fourth Power, a Series A climate and energy technology company founded by Asegun Henry, is evaluated against a peer set of 8 private firms and 1 public comparable in the same sector and adjacent development stages.

Two valuation methods triangulate the company's worth. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the current $150M post-money valuation, implying $300M. Peer-median analysis across 8 climate and energy companies at Series A/B stages yields a median valuation of $305M, with a range spanning $110M to $5.0B; the peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M). Both methods produce closely aligned results.

The combined triangulation supports a valuation range of $255M to $351M, with a median of $302M. This range reflects modest upside to the current $150M valuation, consistent with typical Series A-to-next-stage progression in early-stage frontier energy. Key uncertainties include the significant dispersion in peer valuations (the $5.0B outlier versus the $110M floor) and stage heterogeneity within the comparable set, which limits the precision of peer-derived multiples. Capital efficiency relative to peers and commercialization progress will be material drivers of near-term value realization.
